title: agent-claim-atomic: jjf update --claim sets assignee + in-progress atomically
body:
# Goal

Atomic claim primitive: `jjf update <id> --claim` sets
`assignee=<current jj user>` AND advances status to
`in_progress` in one multi-op commit. Race-free for
parallel agents.

End state: two agents racing `jjf ready --claim --limit 1`
end up holding two DIFFERENT tickets, not the same one.

# Why now

beads (`reference/beads/cmd/bd/`) ships `--claim` as the
agent-loop primitive. Without it, parallel agents pulling
from `jjf ready` race: both grab the same id, both set
themselves as assignee, the bookmark commit ordering decides
one winner silently, the loser duplicates work.

The atomic claim verb is the simplest fix: one commit
carries both the assignee change and the status flip; the
loser's commit conflicts at the bookmark level and they
retry against a fresh `ready` list.

# Scope

## Storage

### New `Status::InProgress` variant

- Extend `Status` enum to `Open | InProgress | Closed`.
- Status precedence stays simple: `open` and `in_progress`
  are both "active"; `closed` is terminal.
- Serde rename to lowercase + hyphen: `in-progress`.
- Default = `Open`.
- This is a spec bump (v2.2 or fold into next bump).
  Existing tickets default to `Open` on read.

### Storage::claim

```rust
impl Storage {
    /// Atomically set assignee and advance status to InProgress.
    /// `who` is the assignee string (typically the jj user.name).
    /// Idempotent: claiming an already-claimed issue by the same
    /// user is a no-op (no commit); claiming by a different user
    /// is an error (`AlreadyClaimed { by: String }`).
    pub fn claim(&self, id: &IssueId, who: &str) -> Result<()>;
}
```

- Implementation: one multi-op commit with `Op::SetAssignee
  { who }` and `Op::SetStatus { InProgress }`.
- Preflight: read current state; if already InProgress with
  same assignee, return Ok without writing; if InProgress
  with different assignee, return `AlreadyClaimed`.

## CLI

- `jjf update <id> --claim` — atomic claim.
- `jjf ready --claim --limit 1` — convenience: pick top
  ready issue and claim in one call. Returns the claimed
  id (text/JSON). If `--limit > 1`, error (claiming
  multiple at once doesn't make sense).
- `--unclaim` on `jjf update`: reverse the claim
  (`unset-assignee` + `set-status open`). Mutually
  exclusive with `--claim` (clap `conflicts_with`).
- `--current-user` is derived from `jj config get user.name`
  (same source the trailer infra already uses).

### `jjf ready` semantics under the new status

- By default, `jjf ready` excludes `InProgress` (claimed
  issues are "taken"; an idle agent shouldn't see them).
- `--include-claimed` flag: include InProgress in results.
  Use case: "show me what's in flight."

## Spec

- `docs/storage-format.md`:
  - §3 `IssueRecord.status`: add `in-progress` to the
    enum.
  - §5.7 multi-op create: claim emits set-assignee +
    set-status together.
- `docs/cli-json.md`:
  - `update --claim` envelope.
  - `ready --claim --limit 1` envelope.
  - New error kind `already_claimed` with `details.by:
    <assignee>`.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `jjf update <id> --claim` sets assignee = current
  user AND status = in-progress in one commit.
- [ ] Re-claiming by same user: no-op, exit 0.
- [ ] Re-claiming by different user: `already_claimed`
  error (exit 2).
- [ ] `jjf update <id> --unclaim` unsets assignee + sets
  status open.
- [ ] `jjf ready` excludes InProgress by default.
- [ ] `jjf ready --include-claimed` includes them.
- [ ] `jjf ready --claim --limit 1` picks + claims atomically;
  output is the claimed id.
- [ ] Two parallel `ready --claim --limit 1` calls do NOT
  collide (one wins, the other gets the next id; written
  as an integration test).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` stays green.

# Out of scope

- A `--ttl` on the claim ("auto-unclaim after 30 min").
  Separate ticket if claims drift.
- Multi-user negotiation (queue, preemption, priority
  override). v1 is first-write-wins.
- A `jjf release <id>` alias for `--unclaim`. The flag
  is fine.
- Web-UI / TUI claim browser.

# Pointers

- Reference impl: beads' `bd update --claim` (`reference/beads/cmd/bd/update.go`).
- Status enum: `crates/jjf-storage/src/record.rs` (current
  Open/Closed shape).
- Op precedent: `Op::SetStatus`, `Op::SetAssignee` in
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/op.rs`.
- Resolver: existing scalar LWW handles status and
  assignee correctly already.
